project.name: Shepherd Text Coaching Agent
project.description: AI-powered text coaching agent for Denver Griffin's Divorce Stoppers program. Uses persistent memory, screenshot analysis, and Denver's coaching methodology.

phases.name: Webinar CTA Platform
phases.notes: Research complete: video.js overlays, $25/mo vs $299/mo. CTA at 81 min hits 25% → 45 min doubles audience.

phases.name: MPMC Scale (450 Coaches)
phases.notes: If Shepherd works for Denver → template it for 450 coaches at low marginal cost.

contradictions.label: Revenue vs Affordability
contradictions.detail: Claims $700-800K/month peak but can't afford $2,500. Treat $100K/month as ceiling.

contradictions.label: Solo + Queue Paradox
contradictions.detail: Running 40-50 clients alone with 4,500 in queue. Bandwidth collapse is the real pain.

contradictions.label: Scope vs Payment
contradictions.detail: $2K buys a taste, not a meal. Written deliverables needed.

contradictions.label: Previous AI Failed
contradictions.detail: Almost perfect agent broke ~4 months ago. Likely prompt drift.

calls.preview: March 18, 2026 — Glassy Intro Call Summary Duration: ~2h 30m Participants: Glassy (new sales partner), Mike Bitler, Alex Mazzei, Ely Beckman Purpose: Introduction, alignment check, scope the commercial relationship Recording: iPhone transcription → Meridian processing --- Participants Glassy (New)

calls.title: march18
calls.preview: March 18 Call — File Index Date: 2026-03-18 Call: Alex + Ely (Glassy) + Bitler (~2.5 hours) Raw transcript: `../march 18 glassy intro bitler.md` > Note: Glassy = Ely Beckman. They are the same person. The call was Alex and

decisions.why: Same SHA-256 gate as existing dashboards. Read-only view. Prevents casual discovery.

decisions.what: meridian-demos as GitHub Pages deploy target
decisions.why: Pages enabled, public repo. Private repo (meridian) can't use Pages on free plan.

decisions.what: Pre-bake model replaces browser fetch for mesh data
decisions.why: GitHub Pages can't reach Tailscale IPs. Mesh data baked at generation time via SSH/cron.
Denver agreed to pay. Invoice sent via PayPal to Merchant Business Systems.

decisions.what: API-first model — plug into Go High Level, don't rip and replace
decisions.why: Build trust first. Migrate platforms later. Denver already on GHL at $300/mo.

decisions.what: Data sovereignty — brain on MERIDIAN infra, not client hardware
decisions.why: Client keeps data/results but not the engine. Prevents IP leak.

decisions.what: Client data in main repo (not per-client repos) until 5+ clients
decisions.why: Per-client repos add operational overhead. Directory in monorepo sufficient at 3 clients.

decisions.what: self-hosted runner for GitHub Actions (not ubuntu-latest)
decisions.why: Private repo on free plan can't use GitHub-hosted runners. Self-hosted runners currently offline.

decisions.what: Progressive enhancement — Gateway API live, dashboard-data.json static fallback
decisions.why: Dashboard works everywhere: Tailscale (live), GitHub Pages (static), customer VPS (their Gateway).

decisions.what: React 18 CDN for Command Center (same as meshdash.html)
decisions.why: Single file, no build step, component reuse from 4 existing React pages.

decisions.what: Option C (cron push from prime) for mesh metrics
decisions.why: Simplest. No new services. Decoupled freshness. Existing autopull pattern.

decisions.what: Glassy PC added to Tailscale mesh
decisions.why: 32GB RAM, i7, 11TB storage. GPU compute + backup sync node.

decisions.what: Denver maps to Tier 2 (Intelligence OS) — cloud-hosted exoskeleton
decisions.why: Dedicated containerized instance, private document ingestion, custom persona. Upgrade to T3 if sovereignty demands escalate.

calls.title: At Bitler's house — Denver review
calls.summary: In-person meeting with Denver Griffin at Bitler's house. Denver agreed to $2K retainer for text coaching AI agent. He had an 'almost perfect' AI that broke 4 months ago. Solo operator with zero employees. Wants to own all infrastructure. MPMC vision: clone coaching brains into 1,000 AI businesses.
decisions: Text message coaching AI agent as first deliverable
Denver provides program access, transcripts, David's roadmap
Performance-based pricing proposed for ongoing engagement

Build text message coaching agent MVP in two weeks
Invoice $2K via PayPal and coordinate data handoff

discoveries: Denver had 'almost perfect' text coaching agent — screenshot analysis, coached response, revised message
Previous AI used Claude, two-tiered memory system — broke when David tweaked it
Zero employees — fired 16-person team including David
400-500 MPMC coach applications — scale play if text agent works
Denver records everything — years of transcripts as training data
Wants 3-4 redundant backup locations, data sovereignty non-negotiable
ManyChat as stopgap — knows it's not AI
